failure_reason: D104 ce_authority_ts elimination rejected: VC message ts field would carry session value creating semantic inconsistency. ce_authority_ts appears in P6/P17 as semantic constraint on authority endorsement. CE-only elimination without broader refactor leaves spec worse.

proposal.claim: ce_authority_ts is provably equal to ce_primary (both init to 1, both increment by 1 only in CEViewChange). Eliminating ce_authority_ts removes a redundant state variable, reducing state space and clarifying that under TEE trust the session number IS the monotonic authority counter.

key_findings: Redundancy proof is mechanically correct: ce_authority_ts = ce_primary is an inductive invariant
VC message record (spec:317-320) carries both ts and session as distinct fields; after merge ts |-> new_session creates a protocol-level naming lie
ce_authority_ts appears in INV6 (line 893) and INV17 (lines 971-972) as semantic constraints, not just type invariants
authority_ts / primary separation is a consistent pattern across CE/OB/OMS encoding trust model domain fact #4
CE-only elimination without OB/OMS creates inconsistent modeling pattern

judgment.ruling: Reviewer sustained. Proposal D104 on C1 rejected.
judgment.decisive_factor: VC message structure (spec:317-320) already distinguishes ts (authority) from session (primary) as separate record fields. Setting ts |-> new_session creates a semantic mismatch at the message level. ce_authority_ts is the spec's only formal representation of authority endorsement (domain fact #4); eliminating it severs traceability to the trust model.
judgment.conditions_for_reversal: ACCEPT if and only if: (1) VC message ts field also renamed/collapsed, (2) INV6 and INV17 reworded to reference session numbers explicitly, (3) authority_ts eliminated uniformly across CE/OB/OMS.
